title: Tailwind CSS v4 — What Changed and Why It Matters
content:
Tailwind CSS v4 dropped and it's a complete rewrite. Here's what you need to know.

No More config File

The tailwind.config.js is gone. Everything is configured through CSS imports and @theme directives. Less files, less confusion.

@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  --color-accent: #e8ff47;
  --font-display: 'Syne', sans-serif;
}

Faster Compilation

The new engine is written in Rust and compiles styles up to 5x faster. On large projects, this means near-instant rebuilds.

Container Queries Built In

No more plugins. Container queries are now native to Tailwind:

<div class="@container">
  <div class="@sm:text-lg @lg:text-xl">
    Responsive text
  </div>
</div>

Should You Upgrade?

If you're starting a new project, absolutely. For existing projects, the migration is mostly removing the config file and updating imports. Most utility classes work the same.

The future of CSS frameworks is here, and it's blazing fast.
